reforms.description: The Czech government aims to enhance its science and research capabilities by building on existing human capital and innovative potential, while also planning to boost programmes for green R&D, particularly focusing on low-carbon technologies, to enhance its research capabilities in this critical area.
reforms.package_name: Human Capital and Green R&D Enhancement
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing human capital and focusing on green R&D are expected to raise productivity and innovation in the Czech economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced plans to enhance human capital and boost green R&D programmes.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms are significant but not structural, focusing on enhancing existing capabilities and funding.
reforms.source_quote: Key priorities of the current government include... Science and research: building on the existing high-quality human capital and innovative potential to further support science and research.

reforms.description: The THÉTA programme and the Czech RE:START strategy aim to enhance green innovative capacity and support economic restructuring in coal regions by focusing on applied experimental research, entrepreneurship, and investment in green technologies, with significant funding allocated from both national and European sources.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These strategies are expected to foster innovation and economic growth in transitioning regions by investing in green technologies and entrepreneurship.
reforms.status_evidence: The THÉTA programme and RE:START strategy have been launched and are currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a significant structural change in supporting green innovation and economic restructuring.
reforms.source_quote: In September 2022, the European Commission approved CZK 40 billion (1.64 billion EUR) through the Just Transition Fund to support the transition to green energy in the Czech coal regions.

reforms.description: The 2030+ education strategy emphasizes reforming the Vocational Education and Training (VET) sector by modernizing curricula, postponing specialization, and increasing employer involvement in curriculum development to better align education with the needs of the labor market.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Modernizing vocational education is expected to enhance the skills of the workforce, thereby supporting innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The 2030+ education strategy has been officially launched and is currently being implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a major overhaul of the vocational education system, which is critical for aligning skills with market needs.
reforms.source_quote: The 2030+ education strategy puts emphasis on reforming the Vocational Education and Training (VET) sector.

reforms.description: The Czech government has established subsidy programmes to support research, development, and innovation among small and medium-sized enterprises, covering a significant portion of costs for energy efficiency improvements.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Supporting SMEs in R&D and innovation is expected to enhance their competitiveness and contribute to overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The subsidy programmes have been established and are currently operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of subsidy programmes is significant but does not represent a structural change.
reforms.source_quote: Subsidies for research, development and innovation among small and medium-sized enterprises are available from the Operational Programme for Technologies and Applications for Competitiveness (OPTAK).

reforms.description: The Czech government has initiated agreements with various funds to invest in innovative start-ups and emerging growth-oriented companies, addressing the lack of venture capital in the market.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in start-ups is expected to stimulate innovation and economic growth by providing necessary capital to emerging companies.
reforms.status_evidence: Agreements with funds have been initiated and are currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative is significant in addressing venture capital shortages but does not represent a major structural change.
reforms.source_quote: To help remedy the lack of venture capital, the Czech government has concluded agreements with various funds that may invest in innovative start-ups.
